source_refs.snippet:
commit 584f396132aa19d21bb1e38ad9a5d428869290cb
Author:     Reimar Döffinger <Reimar.Doeffinger@gmx.de>
AuthorDate: Mon Jul 6 09:32:17 2020 +0200
Commit:     Guo, Yejun <yejun.guo@intel.com>
CommitDate: Mon Jul 6 20:22:30 2020 +0800

    dnn_backend_native: Add overflow check for length calculation.
    
    We should not silently allocate an incorrect sized buffer.
    Fixes trac issue #8718.
    
    Signed-off-by: Reimar Döffinger <Reimar.Doeffinger@gmx.de>
    Reviewed-by: Michael Niedermaye

commit 656cb0450aeb73b25d7d26980af342b37ac4c568
Author:     Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>
AuthorDate: Tue Feb 15 17:58:08 2022 +0800
Commit:     Peter Ross <pross@xvid.org>
CommitDate: Fri Feb 18 16:54:11 2022 +1100

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
    
    Since the av_malloc() may fail and return NULL pointer,
    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked
    whether the new allocation is success.
    
    Fixes: d14723861b ("VP3: fix decoding of vid

commit d254fe2d1da3b1cba3526c5d6417c9912e330988
Author:     Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
AuthorDate: Thu Oct 19 22:07:36 2023 +0200
Commit:     Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
CommitDate: Sun Apr 14 22:12:33 2024 +0200

    avformat/rtsp: Use rtsp_st->stream_index
    
    Fixes: out of array access
    Fixes: rtpdec_h264.c149/poc
    
    Found-by: Hardik Shah of Vehere
    Reviewed-by: Martin Storsjö <martin@martin.st>
    Signed-off-by: Michael Niedermayer <michae

commit e4d5ac8d7d2a08658b3db7dd821246fe6b35381f
Author:     Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
AuthorDate: Thu Oct 19 22:07:36 2023 +0200
Commit:     Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
CommitDate: Fri Oct 27 18:10:47 2023 +0200

    avformat/rtsp: Use rtsp_st->stream_index
    
    Fixes: out of array access
    Fixes: rtpdec_h264.c149/poc
    
    Found-by: Hardik Shah of Vehere
    Reviewed-by: Martin Storsjö <martin@martin.st>
    Signed-off-by: Michael Niedermayer <michae

content.output:
commit 656cb0450aeb73b25d7d26980af342b37ac4c568
Author: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>
Date:   Tue Feb 15 17:58:08 2022 +0800

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
    
    Since the av_malloc() may fail and return NULL pointer,
    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked
    whether the new allocation is success.
    
    Fixes: d14723861b ("VP3: fix decoding of videos with stride > 2048")
    Reviewed-by: Peter Ross <pross@xvid.org>
    Signed-off-by: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>

 libavcodec/vp3.c | 7 ++++++-
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

commit 656cb0450aeb73b25d7d26980af342b37ac4c568
Author: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>
Date:   Tue Feb 15 17:58:08 2022 +0800

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
    
    Since the av_malloc() may fail and return NULL pointer,
    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc

    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked
    whether the new allocation is success.
    
    Fixes: d14723861b ("VP3: fix decoding of videos with stride > 2048")
    Reviewed-by: Peter Ross <pross@xvid.org>
    Signed-off-by: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>

 libavcodec/vp3.c | 7 ++++++-
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

content.claim: git log --follow around changed path libavcodec/vp3.c
content.snippet:
656cb0450a avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
f025b8e110 avcodec/threadframe: Add ff_thread_(get|release)_ext_buffer()
b3551b6072 avcodec/thread: Move ff_thread_(await|report)_progress to new header
46309f262c avcodec/vp3: Don't output bogus warning
96caa01f13 avcodec/vp3: Check version in all cases when VP4 code is not built

content.pattern: if (!s->edge_emu_buffer) {...} s->edge_emu_buffer = av_malloc(...); if (!s->edge_emu_buffer) { ret = AVERROR(ENOMEM); goto error; }

content.description: s->edge_emu_buffer is assigned the return value of av_malloc() without any NULL check on the allocation result; if av_malloc returns NULL, s->edge_emu_buffer is NULL and will be dereferenced later.

After av_malloc() is called, the result assigned to s->edge_emu_buffer is checked for NULL. If NULL, ret is set to AVERROR(ENOMEM) and control jumps to the error label, preventing subsequent NULL pointer dereference.
The av_malloc() call is only reached when s->edge_emu_buffer is already NULL (the outer if (!s->edge_emu_buffer) check existed before the fix), implementing lazy allocation.
av_malloc() returns a non-NULL pointer (memory allocation succeeds), which would make the NULL check pass and execution proceeds normally without triggering the vulnerability.

content.summary: The return value of av_malloc() assigned to s->edge_emu_buffer in vp3_decode_frame() is not checked for NULL before the pointer is used, causing a NULL pointer dereference when memory allocation fails.
content.mechanism: In function vp3_decode_frame (libavcodec/vp3.c, line ~2683), s->edge_emu_buffer is lazily allocated via av_malloc(9 * FFABS(s->current_frame.f->linesize[0])) inside a block that only guards whether the buffer pointer is already NULL. If av_malloc fails and returns NULL, s->edge_emu_buffer becomes NULL, but execution continues past the allocation without error handling. Subsequent code paths that dereference s->edge_emu_buffer (e.g., edge emulation loops) will trigger a NULL pointer dereference, matching CWE-476.

content.description: CWE-476 (NULL Pointer Dereference) from the CVE context aligns with the observed missing NULL check on av_malloc, consistent with the patch evidence but not used as root-cause proof.

content.reason: The single fix commit patch only shows the addition of the NULL check, but the precise downstream dereference site of s->edge_emu_buffer that triggers the actual NULL pointer dereference is not visible in the patch hunk itself.
Only one FixCommit is present in the packet, so the analysis is based on a single change site without cross-checking equivalent backports or cherry-picks.

git log --follow around changed path libavformat/rtsp.c
content.snippet:
d254fe2d1d avformat/rtsp: Use rtsp_st->stream_index
ea9418debc avformat/rtsp: break on unknown protocols
d1e8be3411 Revert "avformat/rtp: Pass sources and block filter addresses via sdp file for rtp"
635ca9aa01 avformat/rtp: Pass sources and block filter addresses via sdp file for rtp
289838b7bd libavformat/rtsp: return error if rtsp_hd_out is null instead of crash

content.description: The loop variable 'i' in parse_fmtp() is passed to rtsp_st->dynamic_handler->parse_sdp_a_line(s, i, ...) as the stream index, but 'i' is an iteration counter over rt->nb_rtsp_streams, not the actual stream index in s->streams[].
rtsp_st->stream_index replaces 'i' as the second argument to parse_sdp_a_line(), providing the correct stream index within s->streams[].

The call site is guarded by rtsp_st->sdp_payload_type == payload_type, rtsp_st->dynamic_handler non-null, and rtsp_st->dynamic_handler->parse_sdp_a_line non-null. None of these guards validate that the passed stream index 'i' is within bounds of s->streams[].

The loop index 'i' is not guaranteed to equal rtsp_st->stream_index. The RTSP stream table may have gaps or differing ordering from s->streams[], so 'i' can differ from the correct stream position inside AVFormatContext.

content.summary: In parse_fmtp(), the loop index variable 'i' is passed to parse_sdp_a_line() as a stream-index argument, but 'i' iterates over the RTSP stream table and does not equal the stream's real index in AVFormatContext->streams[]. Downstream dynamic handlers that use this value as an array index can read out-of-bounds.
content.mechanism: parse_fmtp loops over rt->nb_rtsp_streams with counter i. For each RTSPStream that matches payload_type and has a dynamic_handler with parse_sdp_a_line set, the code invokes parse_sdp_a_line(s, i, ...). However dynamic handler implementations (e.g. rtpdec_h264.c) use the second parameter as an index into s->streams[]. The loop variable i does not equal rtsp_st->stream_index — it merely counts iterations through the rtsp_streams table. When i >= s->nb_streams, the handler performs an out-of-bounds array access, as reported in the commit ("Fixes: out of array access / rtpdec_h264.c149/poc").

content.description: The two fix commits are a cherry-pick pair (original e4d5ac8d + backport d254fe2d1), so the vulnerability existed in at least two release branches.

content.reason: The full parse_sdp_a_line handler implementations inside rtpdec_h264.c or other dynamic handlers are not in the provided evidence; the exact out-of-bounds target array is inferred from the commit message 'rtpdec_h264.c149/poc' and the function signature change.
It is not confirmed from the provided evidence how rtsp_st entries relate to s->streams[] ordering — the mismatch between loop counter i and rtsp_st->stream_index is inferred from the fix semantics.

  */

content.pattern: uint64_t len = sizeof(float); for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) { len *= oprd->dims[i]; if (len > INT32_MAX) return 0; } return len;
// NOTE: User must check for error (return value <= 0) to handle // case like integer overflow.

content.description: calculate_operand_data_length() performs int32_t multiplication (oprd->dims[0] * oprd->dims[1] * oprd->dims[2] * oprd->dims[3] * sizeof(float)) without overflow checking, allowing the product to wrap around to a small positive value

Callers of calculate_operand_data_length() use the returned length directly with av_malloc/av_realloc without validating that the value is positive and sane, then proceed to write data into the allocated region assuming it is large enough
calculate_operand_data_length() rewritten to use uint64_t accumulator and stepwise multiplication with overflow guard (if len > INT32_MAX return 0), preventing integer wrap-around
All callers of calculate_operand_data_length() now check (length <= 0) before using the value for memory allocation, returning error when overflow is detected
Overflow guard: after each dimension multiplication, check if len > INT32_MAX, and if so return 0 to signal error to callers
Before fix: no overflow check existed in calculate_operand_data_length(); no length <= 0 validation existed in any caller site

content.summary: Integer overflow in calculate_operand_data_length() leads to undersized heap buffer allocation and subsequent out-of-bounds write
content.mechanism: calculate_operand_data_length() in libavfilter/dnn/dnn_backend_native.c computed buffer length by multiplying four user-controlled dimension values and sizeof(float) using int32_t arithmetic without overflow checks. Large dimension values cause integer overflow, producing a small (truncated) length value. Callers use this truncated length with av_malloc/av_realloc to allocate undersized buffers, then write beyond the allocated region, resulting in heap buffer overflow (CWE-120).

content.description: CWE-120 context aligns with heap buffer overflow from undersized allocation but cannot serve as root-cause proof
The patch touches 8 files with the same guard pattern; all call sites are mechanically equivalent backports of the same fix within a single commit — no ambiguity about which commit is the fix

content.reason: The patch evidence clearly shows the before/after of calculate_operand_data_length and all callers. No observed uncertainty about the code-level mechanism.
